records.ety: [L. stagnatus, p. p. of stagnare to stagnate, make stagnant, from stagnum a piece of standing water. See Stank a pool, and cf. Stanch, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Stagnated (-n*td); p. pr. & vb. n. Stagnating.]

defs: To cease to flow; to be motionless; as, blood stagnates in the veins of an animal; hence, to become impure or foul by want of motion; as, air stagnates in a close room.
To cease to be brisk or active; to become dull or inactive; as, commerce stagnates; business stagnates.
quotes: Ready-witted tenderness . . . never stagnates in vain lamentations while there is any room for hope.
